items.title: Violence erupts at right-wing demonstration in the Netherlands ahead of election
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A police vehicle was set alight as demonstrators in a right-wing protest in the Netherlands erupted into violence and chaos on Saturday. The unrest comes just weeks before the country holds a general election.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Passengers react as cyberattack disrupts check-in systems at major European airports
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The disruptions to electronic systems initially reported at Brussels, Berlin's Brandenburg and London's Heathrow airports meant that only manual check-in and boarding was possible. Many other European airports said their operations were unaffected.

Read more: https://apnews.com​

items.title: British couple held for months in Afghanistan on undisclosed charges arrive back in UK
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Taliban released the British couple held in Afghanistan for more than seven months on undisclosed charges, likely part of a wider effort to get their government recognised internationally years after taking power.

items.title: Many wounded as Russian strikes hit Ukraine’s Dnipropetrovsk region
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said attacks took place across nine regions, including Dnipropetrovsk, Mykolaiv, Chernihiv, Zaporizhzhia, Poltava, Kyiv, Odesa, Sumy and Kharkiv. At least 30 people were wounded in the attack in Ukraine's central Dnipropetrovsk region, local governor Serhii Lysak said. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Residents flee Gaza City as Israel expands ground offensive
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Many have been attempting to relocate from the city, where 1 million people once lived, to the southern Gaza Strip following Israeli military calls for a full evacuation. Hundreds of thousands remained in the city, the territory’s largest and already in ruins from nearly two years of war and struggling with famine. 


Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Airports across Europe says technical issue hit check-in, boarding systems
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The attack has affected Collins, which provides the technology that allows passengers to check themselves in, print boarding passes and bag tags, and dispatch their own luggage from a kiosk. Airports advised travelers to check their flight status and apologized for any inconvenience.

items.title: Crowds stream into Oktoberfest grounds as Munich beer festival opens
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The Munich festival runs for 16 days, from September 20 to October 5, and typically draws around six million visitors. Beer prices this year range between 14.50 and 15.80 euros per liter.

items.title: Hong Kong police defuse World War II bomb found at construction site
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The discovery was made in Quarry Bay, a popular residential and business district on the west of Hong Kong island. Thousands of local residents were evacuated from the area as a disposal team brought in sandbags and specialist equipment for the defusal.

items.content:
<article>Live view of Kilauea, Hawaii’s most active volcano. The volcano on the Big Island of Hawaii has been erupting on-and-off since December.

items.title: Man accused of trying to kill Trump says case not proven
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A man accused of trying to assassinate President Donald Trump at his Florida golf course last year told a federal judge on Friday that prosecutors haven’t proven that an assassination attempt occurred.

items.title: Mexico’s army trains 143 Haitian soldiers as Haiti ramps up fight against gangs
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A group of 143 Haitian soldiers on Friday finished a military training session by the Mexican army as part of a bilateral agreement as Haiti is looking to revive its armed forces amid the escalating violence in the Caribbean nation. (AP Video: Fernanda Pesce)
